{"step_by_step_thinking": "The intensifying screen is used in screen-film radiography to enhance the visibility of the image by converting the x-ray energy into light, which is then captured on the film. In extraoral radiographs, the intensifying screen helps to improve the contrast and detail of the image, making it easier to visualize the structures being examined. The other options, such as decreasing radiation to the patient or collimation, are not the primary function of the intensifying screen.", "answer_choice": "B"}
